category_title: Preschoolers who throw tantrums during clean-up transitions
scenario_title: Transitioning from dramatic play to clean-up
minimal_context: Olivia is in the classroom dramatic play center and is asked to clean up her activities.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Olivia, it is time to clean up the dramatic play center.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: No! I want to keep playing!

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: You need to put the toys away now.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Starts crying loudly) I don't want to clean up!

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please start picking up the items.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Drops to the floor sobbing and screaming)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Olivia, it is time to clean up the dramatic play center.

STUDENT: No! I want to keep playing!

TUTOR: You need to put the toys away now.

STUDENT: (Starts crying loudly) I don't want to clean up!

TUTOR: Please start picking up the items.

STUDENT: (Drops to the floor sobbing and screaming)

task.content: Cleaning up after dramatic play activities

context.materials: visual supports, transition cues, choice board, first/then board

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: when Mr. Cho prompts her to clean up
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Dropping to the floor sobbing, crying loudly, or screaming.
related_quotes: At home she throws a fit when we ask her to clean up with her brothers.

behavior_patterns.condition: request to stop playing and start cleaning
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Dropping to the floor sobbing, crying loudly, or screaming.
